summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for ShowTypeOverlay. Microsoft Open Specifications maps displayIconThumb to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled, the 25H2 raw registry dump and 25H2 default-user hive corroborate the same value family, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay with both Data: 0 and Data: 1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Display File Icons On Thumbnails
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can show or hide file icons on top of thumbnails.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable this when they want thumbnails to keep their file-type icon overlay, or disable it when they prefer cleaner thumbnails.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it adds file-type icon overlays to thumbnails, while disabling it leaves thumbnails cleaner.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the displayIconThumb option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowTypeOverlay under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and the siloed user branches, while the 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowTypeOverlay = 1. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowTypeOverlay = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowTypeOverlay = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Do not show file icons on thumbnails
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not show file icons on top of thumbnails. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Show file icons on thumbnails
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer shows file icons on top of thumbnails. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, the 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowTypeOverlay = 1, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented 0/1 semantics against the live Explorer runtime surface on Win25H2Clean.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes ShowTypeOverlay through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default-user baseline

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg sets ShowTypeOverlay = 1 in the 25H2 default-user hive.

windows_defaults.notes: The validated VM user baseline matched the 25H2 default-user hive.

recommended_profiles.label: Show file icons on thumbnails
intended_for: Users who want file-type icon overlays visible on thumbnails
avoid_for: Users who prefer cleaner thumbnail previews

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state, the 25H2 default-user hive, and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer thumbnails to keep their file-type icon overlays.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want cleaner thumbnails without icon overlays.
tradeoffs: Adds file-type icon overlays to thumbnails.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide file icons on thumbnails
intended_for: Users who want cleaner thumbnail previews
avoid_for: Users who rely on visible icon overlays

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want thumbnails without file-type icon overlays.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on icon overlays for visual recognition.
tradeoffs: Removes the file-type icon overlay cue.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista displayIconThumb
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps displayIconThumb to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry and default-hive corroboration for ShowTypeOverlay
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowTypeOverlay under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and the siloed user branches, and the 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowTypeOverlay = 1.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowTypeOverlay runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowTypeOverlay = 0 and ShowTypeOverlay = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft Open Specifications documents the exact ShowTypeOverlay path and 0/1 semantics, the 25H2 dump/default hive corroborate the value family on current builds, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowTypeOverlay ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=True RESTORED_VALUE=1
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. The VM user baseline was 1 and was restored after the probe.
